summary: Critical IT incidents nearly tripled from FY22 to FY23 (61 to 163) as OTI, newly consolidated from several separate city tech offices, took on many more IT services and saw a roughly one-third jump in incident workload; OTI itself flagged dependence on third-party telecom and cloud vendors as a driver. Incidents then declined in FY25 as OTI credited hardware/software upgrades and a cloud-services migration for the drop.
oneLiner: increasing overall, though upgrades and a cloud migration cut incidents recently
